tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephropathy. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes such as diabetes or glomerular disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
